Q: Why is the library named after Cyril and Methodius?
A: Based on the visual evidence in the video, the library is named after Cyril and Methodius to honor their historical significance, which is prominently displayed by the large monument of the two saints standing in front of the building. Saints Cyril and Methodius are revered as the creators of the first Slavic alphabet (the Glagolitic script), which formed the basis for the Cyrillic script. Naming the National Library after them is a tribute to their foundational contribution to the written language, literacy, and culture of the nation.
C: The brothers Cyril and Methodius invented the first Bulgarian alphabet which is why the library is named after them.
